hero.headline: Regenerative spaces for people and planet.
hero.subStatement: Parti Design is a multidisciplinary architecture and design studio. We develop regenerative places through digital, physical and social systems.

digital.label: Digital Design
digital.heading: Platforms and tools that bring people together
digital.body: We build the digital infrastructure communities need — websites, platforms, and tools that make participation possible and organisations more effective.

physical.label: Architecture
physical.heading: Buildings that communities can own and shape
physical.body: We design and build spaces rooted in participation — from architectural concepts to WikiHouse construction and byggemenskap coordination. Every project begins with the people who will use it.

social.label: Regenerative Strategies
social.heading: Designing with living systems, not against them
social.body: We work with holistic and living systems principles — leveraging the story of place to restore vibrant cultures, ecologies, and communities.

architecturePage.pageDescription: We design and build spaces rooted in participation — from architectural concepts to WikiHouse construction and byggemenskap coordination.
architecturePage.heading: Buildings that communities can own and shape
architecturePage.intro: Our architecture practice is rooted in participation. We work with communities, municipalities, and organisations to design buildings and spaces where the people who will use them have a genuine say from the start.
architecturePage.body2: We specialise in co-built housing (byggemenskap), WikiHouse construction, and participatory design processes. Many of our projects combine architecture with facilitation and digital tools — because a building alone rarely solves the underlying challenge.

offer.label: Architectural design & planning
offer.body: From concept to planning permission — grounded in the community's needs and aspirations.

offer.label: WikiHouse & co-building
offer.body: Open-source construction systems that let communities build their own spaces with minimal specialist labour.

offer.label: Byggemenskap support
offer.body: End-to-end support for housing cooperatives that want to commission and co-build their own homes.

offer.label: Spatial strategy & consulting
offer.body: Advice on how space can work harder — for municipalities, developers, and community organisations.

cta.heading: Ready to build something together?
cta.body: Tell us about your project and we'll get back to you within a few days.

architecturePage.quote: The best buildings grow from genuine dialogue — not from a designer's vision imposed from above.

steps.label: Listen
steps.body: We start with a discovery session to understand the community, the site, and the goals. No assumptions.

steps.label: Analyse
steps.body: Site visits, context research, and a clear project definition. We map what already exists and what's possible.

steps.label: Design together
steps.body: Iterative design rounds with co-design workshops where the people who will use the space shape the outcome.

steps.label: Build it
steps.body: From planning permission to construction supervision — we see it through to completion.

items.question: Do you work outside Umeå?
items.answer: We work throughout Sweden and take on international projects when the fit is right. Many of our architecture projects have roots in northern Sweden, but we follow the work.

items.question: How long does a project take?
items.answer: A smaller structure like a pavilion or sauna might take 6–12 months. A full byggemenskap project is typically 2–4 years. We're honest about timelines from the start.

items.question: Can you help us set up a byggemenskap?
items.answer: Yes — it's one of our core specialisms. We can support everything from forming the cooperative to designing and co-building the homes.

items.question: What makes your approach different?
items.answer: We treat the people who will use a space as co-designers, not just clients or end-users. This produces buildings that communities actually use, maintain, and care about.

digitalDesignPage.pageDescription: We design and build digital products for communities, public institutions, and social enterprises — from websites to complex community platforms.
digitalDesignPage.heading: Platforms and tools that bring people together
digitalDesignPage.intro: We design and build digital products for communities, public institutions, and social enterprises — from simple, elegant websites to complex community platforms.
digitalDesignPage.body2: Our work covers the full stack: UX strategy, visual design, front-end development, and platform architecture. The common thread in everything we build: the people who use these tools should feel empowered by them.

offer.label: Websites & digital presence
offer.body: Clean, fast, accessible websites that tell your story and convert visitors into collaborators.

offer.label: Community platforms & tools
offer.body: Custom platforms that help communities organise, communicate, and grow.

offer.label: UX & product design
offer.body: Research-led design that puts real users at the centre — from first sketch to shipped product.

offer.label: Digital strategy & consulting
offer.body: Advice on which digital investments will actually move the needle for your organisation.

cta.heading: Got a digital project in mind?
cta.body: Tell us about your project and we'll get back to you within a few days.

digitalDesignPage.quote: A good digital product doesn't just work — it makes the organisation behind it more capable.

steps.label: Discover
steps.body: We map your users, your goals, and the gap between them — through interviews, audits, and honest conversations.

steps.label: Define
steps.body: A clear product brief: what we're building, for whom, and why it will work. Agreed before we write a line of code.

steps.label: Design
steps.body: Wireframes, prototypes, and visual design — iterated with your team until it's right.

steps.label: Build & launch
steps.body: Development, testing, and a clean handover — with documentation so your team can own it.

items.question: Do you build products yourselves?
items.answer: Yes. We handle both design and front-end development in-house. For complex backends or integrations we work with trusted specialist partners.

items.question: We already have a website. Can you improve it?
items.answer: Absolutely. We often take over existing sites and improve them — sometimes incrementally, sometimes with a full redesign. We'll tell you honestly which approach makes sense.

items.question: How do you price digital projects?
items.answer: Most projects are scoped as a fixed-price engagement after a short paid discovery session. We don't do open-ended retainers without a defined scope.

items.question: Do you work with public institutions and non-profits?
items.answer: Yes — they're our most common clients. We understand public procurement, tight budgets, and long decision cycles. We're patient partners.

coDesignPage.pageDescription: We work with holistic and living systems principles — leveraging the story of place to restore vibrant cultures, ecologies and communities.
coDesignPage.heading: Designing with living systems, not against them
coDesignPage.intro: We approach every project through the lens of regenerative design — working with the grain of place rather than imposing on it. By understanding a site's ecological, cultural, and social history, we surface the unique story of place and let it become the generative force behind every decision.
coDesignPage.body2: Rooted in holistic and living systems thinking, our strategies go beyond participation as a technique. They treat communities, ecologies, and cultures as interconnected wholes. The goal is not just better buildings or services — it is the restoration of vibrant, self-sustaining places: communities that flourish, ecologies that regenerate, cultures that have the conditions to thrive.

offer.label: Living systems facilitation
offer.body: Co-design workshops and participatory processes grounded in ecological and social wholeness — surfacing what a place needs to regenerate, not just what clients want to build.

offer.label: Story of place research
offer.body: Deep contextual research into a site's ecological, cultural, and historic layers — the foundation for any regenerative strategy.

offer.label: Placemaking & participatory planning
offer.body: Long-form processes that embed community voice and ecological awareness into planning and development from the earliest stage.

offer.label: Regenerative strategy & consulting
offer.body: Advice on how to align your organisation's work with living systems principles — for municipalities, developers, and community groups.

cta.heading: Want to restore your place?
cta.body: Tell us about your project and we'll get back to you within a few days.

coDesignPage.quote: Every place already knows what it needs. Our job is to help people hear it.

steps.label: Root
steps.body: Deep research into the place: its ecology, history, culture, and the people who inhabit it. We call this the Story of Place.

steps.label: Gather
steps.body: Community gatherings and facilitated workshops that bring the right voices into the conversation — including those who are rarely heard.

steps.label: Weave
steps.body: Synthesising what we've heard into a strategy that is coherent, grounded, and actionable — not just a report.

steps.label: Grow
steps.body: Supporting implementation: connecting strategy to planning processes, grant applications, community governance, and ongoing stewardship.

items.question: What kinds of organisations do you work with?
items.answer: Municipalities, public developers, community organisations, and cultural institutions. Occasionally private landowners with a clear community-facing mission.

items.question: What does a regenerative strategy actually produce?
items.answer: A document alone changes nothing. We produce strategies connected to real decisions — planning processes, grant applications, and community governance. The output is always something actionable.

items.question: How long does a co-design process take?
items.answer: A focused workshop programme is typically 3–6 months. A full regenerative strategy for a larger site or initiative is typically 12–18 months.

items.question: Do you work in Swedish?
items.answer: We work primarily in English. For processes that require Swedish-language facilitation, we bring in trusted local consultants. If language is a concern for your project, just ask — we'll find the right setup.

paradigm.heading: The regenerative paradigm
paradigm.body: Our work is grounded in living systems thinking. We draw on Carol Sanford's Seven First Principles of Regeneration — not as a rigid methodology to follow, but as a lens for understanding what a place needs in order to truly flourish.
paradigm.attribution: Framework: Carol Sanford — Seven First Principles of Regeneration

work.pageDescription: Projects across architecture, digital design, co-building, and community facilitation.

studio.heading: An architecture studio that builds differently.
studio.body1: Founded in 2022 by Kasimir and Karina while still studying — as a vessel for their creative, research, and community interests. The name means three things: participatory design, the French concept diagram that drives an architectural idea, and a refusal to take architecture too seriously.
studio.quote: Citizens are the experts of place.
studio.body2: They work holistically — a single project might combine architecture, workshop facilitation, branding, and a website. The studio operates as both a practice and a venture studio, incubating its own initiatives alongside client work.
studio.photoAlt: Karina and Kasimir — founders of Parti Design

ventures.teaser: Ideas we believe in enough to build ourselves — and in doing so, develop the tools and first-hand experience we bring to every client engagement.
ventures.body: Alongside client work, Parti Design incubates its own projects: initiatives we believe in and build ourselves.
ventures.pageDescription: Alongside client work, Parti Design incubates its own projects: Massvis, Umeå Kallbad, and DIT Egnahem.

byggemenskap.heading: Make your dream home a reality.
byggemenskap.body: Live the way you want: more community, more greenery, more affordable. Parti Design helps you on your way. Discover how building and living together works, join a group, or start your own initiative.

byggemenskapPage.pageDescription: Parti Design helps groups form, co-design, and co-build their own homes in Umeå. Learn about byggemenskap and register your interest in joining a forming group.
byggemenskapPage.heading: Own a home in a community you helped build.
byggemenskapPage.intro: Byggemenskap — community led housing — is one of the most powerful paths to affordable, community-rooted living. Instead of buying into a developer's project, a group of people commission, co-design, and build their own homes together.

valueItems.label: More affordable
valueItems.body: By cutting out the developer margin and making collective decisions, groups consistently achieve lower costs than the open market.

valueItems.label: Shaped by you
valueItems.body: Every decision — layout, materials, common spaces — is made by the people who will live there.

valueItems.label: Stronger community
valueItems.body: Neighbours who build together tend to look out for each other. The process creates social bonds before you even move in.

valueItems.label: More sustainable
valueItems.body: Shared resources, thoughtful material choices, and long-term stewardship — because the residents own the outcome.

byggemenskapPage.codesignBody: Before a group can commit to building together, they need to understand what they're building — and why. We use design thinking methodology to facilitate co-design workshops that bring a forming group into alignment: on values, on aspirations, on the kind of place they want to create together. The result is a cohesive vision that the whole group believes in.
byggemenskapPage.cobuildBody: Nothing builds trust faster than doing something difficult together with your hands. Collaborative building projects — making something real as a group — generate a unique kind of social capital: the kind you feel when you stand back and realise you couldn't have done it alone. We design and facilitate co-building experiences that help forming groups develop the bonds needed to step into the full commitment of building a home together.
byggemenskapPage.opencollectiveHeading: Form your group on Open Collective with Massvis
byggemenskapPage.opencollectiveBody: Set up a project, access fundraising and budgeting tools, and start organising from day one — without forming a legal entity. Föreningen Massvis handles the admin so your group can focus on what matters: building something together.
byggemenskapPage.opencollectiveCta: Register your group on Open Collective
byggemenskapPage.grantHeading: 400,000 SEK to get your project off the ground
byggemenskapPage.grantBody: Boverket offers a dedicated grant for new byggemenskap groups in Sweden. It's designed to fund the planning and design work before construction begins — exactly when groups need support most.
grantItems: An ekonomisk förening or lokalt utvecklingsbolag, with at least 10,000 SEK contributed per member
Ownership or a binding agreement (e.g. an option agreement) on land where the municipality permits residential construction
byggemenskapPage.grantCta: We help groups apply for this grant as part of our early-stage support.
byggemenskapPage.interestHeading: We're forming a group in Umeå
byggemenskapPage.interestBody: Parti Design is actively forming a new byggemenskap group in Umeå. If you're curious — whether you have a vague idea or are ready to commit — register your interest below. We'll be in touch.
byggemenskapPage.interestFormMessage: Tell us a bit about yourself
byggemenskapPage.interestFormMessagePlaceholder: Why does byggemenskap interest you? Do you have a group already? Any connection to land?
byggemenskapPage.interestFormSuccess: Thanks! We'll be in touch soon.
byggemenskapPage.interestFormSuccessBody: We'll reach out once the group starts forming.
byggemenskapPage.interestFormErrorBody: Please try again or email hej@parti.design directly.
byggemenskapPage.institutionalHeading: Scaling community led housing in Sweden
byggemenskapPage.institutionalBody: Sweden's byggemenskap sector is at an early stage. Municipalities, foundations, and development partners who invest now can help shape the model. We work with institutional partners to design processes, develop land strategies, and build the case for community led housing at scale.

closingCta.heading: Let's build something together.
closingCta.body: Whether you have a brief, an idea, or just a question — reach out.

contact.heading: Let's build something together.
contact.body: Whether you have a brief, a rough idea, or just a question — we'd love to hear from you.
contact.byggemenskapHeading: Thinking about building a home together?
contact.byggemenskapBody: We offer end-to-end support for community self-build housing groups — from forming the cooperative to designing and co-building the home. Reach out and let's talk.
contact.replyNote: We usually reply within a couple of days. For urgent matters, email directly.
contact.pageDescription: Get in touch with Parti Design — architecture, digital design, and co-building.

contactForm.messagePlaceholder: Tell us about your project, idea, or question...
contactForm.successBody: We usually reply within a couple of days.
contactForm.errorBody: Please try again or email us directly at hej@parti.design.

home.title: Parti Design — Architecture, Design & Co-building
home.description: A multidisciplinary architecture and design studio developing regenerative places through digital, physical and social systems. Based in Umeå, Sweden.

studio_page.pageDescription: Parti Design is a multidisciplinary architecture and design studio founded in 2022 by Kasimir and Karina in Umeå, Sweden.

header.heading: Work that builds places and communities.
header.body: Projects across architecture, digital design, co-building, and community facilitation — often all at once.

cta.heading: Have a project in mind?
cta.body: We work with municipalities, developers, community organisations, and individuals. Reach out — we'd love to hear about your project.

header.heading: An architecture studio that builds differently.
header.body: Founded in 2022 in Umeå, Sweden. We work at the intersection of architecture, digital design, and community facilitation.

about.heading: The studio.
about.body1a: Parti Design was founded in 2022 by Kasimir and Karina while still studying — as a vessel for their creative, research, and community interests. The name means three things: participatory design, the French
about.body1b: (the concept diagram that drives an architectural idea), and a refusal to take architecture too seriously.
about.quote: Citizens are the experts of place.
about.body2: The studio operates as both a practice and a venture studio — incubating its own initiatives alongside client work. A single project might combine architecture, workshop facilitation, branding, and a website. That's not a coincidence; it's the point.
about.body3: Based in Umeå, Sweden — but we work wherever the project takes us.
about.photoAlt: Karina and Kasimir — founders of Parti Design

team.heading: The team.
team.kasimir: Architecture, digital development, and co-building. Kasimir leads technical design, construction projects, and digital platforms. He has a particular interest in open-source building systems and community land models.
team.karina: Architecture, graphic design, and facilitation. Karina leads visual communication, co-design processes, and spatial design. She brings a sharp eye for aesthetics and a deep commitment to participatory methods.

participatory.label: Participatory
participatory.body: We believe communities are the experts of their own places. Every project begins by listening.

holistic.label: Holistic
holistic.body: A single project might combine architecture, facilitation, branding, and a website. We work across the whole problem.

regenerative.label: Regenerative
regenerative.body: We design for long-term flourishing, not short-term delivery. Buildings and platforms should outlast the brief.

open.label: Open
open.body: We share methods, use open-source tools, and work in public where we can. Good ideas spread further when they're shared.

architecture.label: Architecture
architecture.body: New buildings, spatial interventions, co-building workshops, WikiHouse construction, byggemenskap support.

digital.label: Digital
digital.body: Websites, platforms, digital services, UX/UI design, community tools, and product development.

social.label: Social
social.body: Co-design facilitation, community engagement, placemaking strategy, workshop series, and grant consulting.

cta.heading: Let's work together.
cta.body: Whether you have a brief, a rough idea, or a community problem that needs solving — we'd love to hear from you.

header.heading: Ideas we believe in enough to build ourselves.
header.body: Our ventures are how we stay ahead — testing ideas, building tools, and proving models that make our client work sharper and more credible.

why.heading: Why a venture studio?
why.body1: Client work keeps us sharp, but the things we believe in most — community finance, open-source housing, urban cold bathing — rarely arrive as a brief. So we make them ourselves.
why.body2: Each venture is a real project that we invest our own time, design, and relationships into. Some grow into independent organisations; others fold back into the studio's service offer. All of them sharpen how we think.
why.quote: The best projects we've been part of started with a question, not a budget.

manifesto.heading: Ventures sharpen everything we do.
manifesto.body1: Our ventures are where we go deepest on the problems we care most about — and in doing so, develop the tools, models, and first-hand experience we bring to our services. When we say a community housing process can work, it's because we've run one ourselves.
manifesto.body2: Each venture is a real commitment of our own time, design, and relationships. Some grow into independent organisations. All of them make us better at what we do.

stageModel.heading: How we grow ventures
stageModel.intro: Every venture moves through five stages. Each stage has different needs, different risks, and a different kind of support.

seed.name: Seed
seed.description: Still being shaped. We're testing assumptions, forming the team, and framing the problem.

root.name: Root
root.description: The core offer is taking form — prototyping, designing the pilot, building early partnerships.

sprout.name: Sprout
sprout.description: Live in some real-world form — first users, first site, first service delivered.

grow.name: Grow
grow.description: The model is working and becoming repeatable — traction, rhythm, measurable outcomes.

flourish.name: Flourish
flourish.description: Stable enough to scale, replicate, or spin out — ready for wider impact.

invest.heading: Invest in place.
invest.body: Interested in backing one of our ventures? We're open to conversations with people who want to do something good with their money.

cta.contact: Talk to us about this venture

footer.tagline: Developing regenerative places for people and planet.
